items.title: Melbourne's auction clearance rate at just 50pc as real estate 'takes another hit'

children.content: Melbourne and Sydney record their weakest auction results in years, with fewer than half of all homes going under the hammer finding a buyer.

gallery.altText: An A-frame sign that reads "auction today", directing toward a house.

thumbnail.altText: An A-frame sign that reads "auction today", directing toward a house.

items.title: Thai teenager's father 'deeply saddened' as Australian charged with murder

children.content: The family of a 17-year-old girl in Thailand speak of their grief as an Australian man is charged with her murder.

image.altText: A teary man with a black tshirt slung over his shoulder, speaks into a small microphone as a woman in yellow sits beside him

items.title: AFL accused of putting players at risk as 33 footballers diagnosed with CTE

children.content: Australian Rules footballers who never played in the game's premier league are being diagnosed with CTE, revealing that the brain trauma crisis striking all levels of the game.

image.altText: An AFL player holding the football is tackled to the ground by another player during a game.

items.title: Marija lost her husband five years ago but Meta won't memorialise his Facebook account

children.content: After Marija Milisavljević's husband died in 2021, she wanted to memorialise his Facebook account to keep a trove of photos and videos, but tech giant Meta has not made it easy.

gallery.altText: A phone screen showing a graphic image of a blue flower.
A man and a woman posing for a wedding photo.
A selfie image of a man and a woman.

thumbnail.altText: A phone screen showing a graphic image of a blue flower.
A man and a woman posing for a wedding photo.
A selfie image of a man and a woman.

items.title: Jackson the star as Dockers make it 14 wins in a row

children.content: The Fremantle Dockers keep rolling along, winning their 14th straight game with a dominant victory over Gold Coast after the Kangaroos beat Essendon. Follow live.

image.altText: Luke Jackson celebrates an AFL goal with a Dockers teammate.

items.title: One Nation won Farrer. Winning over Farrer is another matter

children.content: Farrer residents decisively voted to change their once-safe seat. They are now putting Pauline Hanson and One Nation to the test.

image.altText: man and woman cheer in front of an orange backdrop.

items.title: 'A sign of strength': Free help available for new parents facing anxiety

children.content: New parents often struggle in silence, but a growing number of perinatal clinics offering free mental health support are being rolled out across Australia.

gallery.altText: A newborn baby is shown lying down, with one of its feet in the foreground of the image.

thumbnail.altText: A newborn baby is shown lying down, with one of its feet in the foreground of the image.

items.title: Can the Victorian Liberal Party avoid falling apart again?

children.content: The Victorian Liberal Party is competitive in the polls just months out from the November state election. But will party infighting blunt its winning edge?

image.altText: Moira Deeming answering questions from journalists

avatar.alt: Richard Willingham in front of a white background, stares straight down the barrel.

items.title: Described as a 'talented athlete', Hussein Dehaini was gunned down at 33

children.content: Those who knew Hussein Dehaini say he could have been anything he wanted to be. But his life ended at 33, when he was gunned down in the middle of a Melbourne street.

image.altText: Hussein Dehaini pictured in a gym wearing shorts and no top

items.title: Farewell: 27 things Virginia Trioli learned at the ABC

children.content: After almost 40 years in Australian journalism, 27 of them at the ABC, I am now in possession of enough mistakes to claim a small amount of experience.

image.altText: A young Virginia Trioli, wearing a black top with a large white collar, hosts a program

items.title: The sport their boy loved was damaging his brain. 'Now it's too late'

children.content: A talent and a team player, Nick Lowden excelled at Aussie rules. But the knocks, bumps, hits and collisions were adding up.

image.altText: A man and woman sit on a couch in a living room holding a photo of a smiling young man. Their expressions are serious.

items.title: When you buy merch at a gig, bands aren't making as much as you'd think

children.content: Three years after a defining flashpoint in the dispute over merchandise cuts, venues and artists are still at an impasse.

image.altText: A crowd of people stand in front of a merchandise tent for Oasis outside a stadium

items.title: Australia's 15 native duck species face new threat from deadly bird flu

children.content: Australia's native duck species were already threatened by urban sprawl, climate change, hunting and habitat degradation. Now H5N1 bird flu has been discovered on our shores.

image.altText: Three ducks taking flight from a shallow lake.

items.title: Interactive art exhibition encourages visitors to do some colouring in

children.content: An art studio in Ballarat is hosting an interactive exhibition around a colouring book and visitors can contribute to the art.

image.altText: Person standing in front of art wall with colourful images.

items.title: Family left 'shattered', 'traumatised' by woman's murder, court told

children.content: Jessie James Tumaliuan has been sentenced to more than two decades in jail for the violent murder of his estranged wife in March last year.

gallery.altText: Czarina Gatbonton Tumaliuan smiles at the camera standing in a city at night.

thumbnail.altText: Czarina Gatbonton Tumaliuan smiles at the camera standing in a city at night.

avatar.alt: A man with dark skin in a suit looks at the camera

items.title: 'ISIS bride' charged with slavery offences is granted bail

children.content: A court grants bail for Melbourne grandmother Kawsar Ahmad, who is alleged to have owned a slave while in Syria under Islamic State rule.

image.altText: A woman wearing a brown hijab sits in court.

items.title: Taxi company fined following deaths of two passengers in wheelchairs

children.content: A Victorian taxi company has been fined over the separate 2021 deaths of two passengers who used wheelchairs.

image.altText: A minibus and a sedan, both taxis, parked facing each other on some grass.

items.title: CJ wowed audiences on The Piano. Now he's sharing a stage with the MSO

children.content: The Piano taught CJ Jones about friendship and perseverance. He’s now set for his debut on the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra concert stage at the ABC Classic 100 in Concert.

image.altText: A man in a fedora hat and white shirt stands excitedly and smiles at the audiences while playing the piano on stage

items.title: Working with Children Checks not reviewed despite sexual misconduct findings

children.content: Victoria's former children's commissioner didn't refer sexual misconduct cases for a Working with Children Check review, despite saying she would never have done so in cases involving any sexual element "whatsoever".

image.altText: Liana Buchanan has dark glassees and shoulder length brown hair and stands in front of three flags holding a report.

items.title: First Nations midwifery program leads to healthier babies

children.content: First Nations babies born through a midwifery program offered at three Melbourne hospitals are more likely to be full term and a healthy weight, a study has found.

gallery.altText: Smiling new parents with a newborn baby

thumbnail.altText: Smiling new parents with a newborn baby

items.title: Dezi Freeman case leads to police gun carry changes

children.content: Chief Commissioner Mike Bush says Victoria Police officers in the state's alpine region will soon be permitted to carry long-arm rifles after the Dezi Freeman case.

image.altText: A man with white hair in a navy uniform with badges stares off camera.

items.title: Rescuer needs rescuing after fall into deep hole while helping kangaroo

children.content: A wildlife rescue on Raymond Island took a dramatic turn when one of the rescuers found himself trapped underground and needing urgent help.

image.altText: A photo of emergency service workers looking down a hole in bushland, using a ladder to assist their rescue

items.title: Seventy years on, the 'town that moved' isn't going anywhere

children.content: Tallangatta moved from its original position 70 years ago. Those who helped the houses on their way remember the event well.

image.altText: An old black and white image of school kids

items.title: Mother and sister make emotional plea to find Colac man missing for three weeks

children.content: Police say the 43-year-old, who has been missing for three weeks, worked at a tobacco store in Colac.

image.altText: Missing man walkig at train station.

items.title: Long lunches and sickies on the cards as fans prepare for World Cup clash

children.content: The countdown is on for Australia's World Cup match against Paraguay, and some football fans are preparing to happily put work on the backburner.

articles.title: History beckons but a 'fearless' opponent awaits the Socceroos

children.content: Egypt is sweating on the fitness of star forward Mohamed Salah, but the Socceroos won't be complacent as they prepare for just the third FIFA World Cup knockout encounter in the team's history.

image.altText: Forward Mohamed Salah of Egypt talks with midfielder Mohammad Mohebbi of IR Iran

items.title: Man suing Spirit of Tasmania operator told he 'should have sat' on ship's toilet

children.content: A man who claims to have sustained injuries after falling over while using a toilet at sea says the ferry operator was negligent, the federal court has heard.

image.altText: A large white and yellow cruise ship sits in the waterfront of a city.

items.title: Parent asks if 'warning signs' missed after Chinese language school closure

children.content: The shock closure of a Chinese language school after 17 years leaves parents and teachers with "unanswered questions" over the school's financial management.

image.altText: A group of teachers with faces blurred.

items.title: Ex-AFL umpire to stand trial over alleged Brownlow fraud

children.content: Ex-AFL umpire Michael Pell, accused of leaking information on confidential Brownlow votes, has been ordered to stand trial.

image.altText: An image of Michael Pell.

items.title: Qantas axes Alice Springs-Melbourne route amid high cost, falling demand

children.content: Qantas has axed its direct Alice Springs-Melbourne route after "many decades", and will also restrict flights between Darwin and Singapore to the peak tourist season.

image.altText: A photo of a Qantas plane flying in the sky.

items.title: Matthew Guy says he's owed apology over Moira Deeming assault allegation

children.content: Victoria Police close an investigation into allegations state MP Matthew Guy assaulted fellow Liberal Moira Deeming at a community event, determining there was "there was no offence detected".

gallery.altText: A man in a suit stands looking sad.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a suit stands looking sad.

items.title: House prices in two Australian capitals tipped to fall substantially

children.content: House price falls of up to 7 and 8 per cent in Sydney and Melbourne have been forecast for the year ahead, according to Domain. But it is far from doom and gloom for existing owners, with resale profits hitting a record.

image.altText: A sign in front of a building that reads 'Sold'

items.title: Residents in regional Victoria hire security patrols over crime fears

children.content: About 20 residents in the Victorian city of Ballarat are paying a security company to patrol their streets as a "crime deterrent".

image.altText: A car at night with flashing lights.

items.title: Housing fix in town where mining and medical workers can't find rentals

children.content: The first stage of a $9 million private worker accommodation development, designed to make it easier to attract essential workers to western Victoria, has reached completion.

image.altText: The interior of an appartment

items.title: When can gambling become an addiction? Here are the warning signs

children.content: Experts say the gambling industry is increasingly targeting women, and there are potential risks with a novelty bet.
